title: Within-lap ranking expert using race-lap query groups
summary: Pit calls are relative: on a given lap, only some cars are truly "most urgent" to pit.
rationale: Pit calls are relative: on a given lap, only some cars are truly "most urgent" to pit. CatBoost and LightGBM ranking objectives support grouped pairwise/query learning, which fits the idea of ranking drivers within the same `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` state rather than predicting each row independently.
implementation_hint: Train a ranker with `group_id=(Year,Race,LapNumber)` and relevance=`PitNextLap`, optionally restricting pair generation to nearby positions or same compound/stint buckets. Blend the ranker score with the existing binary model; do not replace the classifier outright.
expected_effect: Improves ordering within the same race context, which is directly aligned with ROC AUC and can capture undercut/overcut urgency better than absolute features alone.
risk: Many groups will have zero or one positive, so the ranker can be noisy unless grouped carefully and regularized; needs validation against a binary baseline.
